site.name: adidas Ireland
site.mechanism_justification: adidas.ie uses a custom ecommerce platform (likely SAP Commerce Cloud / Salesforce Commerce Cloud) with significant anti-bot protection. Direct HTTP and standard Playwright are blocked; only UC Chrome (undetected Chrome) can access the site. The page returns rich JSON-LD (ProductGroup schema) and likely has internal API endpoints. The site has an internal API at /api/products/{code} that returns product data (confirmed 68K+ chars response). Two approaches available: (1) Parse the rendered HTML page for JSON-LD and CSS selectors, or (2) Use the internal /api/products/{code} endpoint for structured JSON data.

anti_bot.details: The site blocks direct HTTP requests and standard Playwright browser. Only UC Chrome (undetected Chrome) successfully loads pages. This is consistent with Akamai Bot Manager protection used by adidas globally. The anti-bot system requires sophisticated browser fingerprinting to bypass.

connectivity.notes: UC Chrome (undetected) without proxy is sufficient to access the site. Standard Playwright is blocked. Direct HTTP is blocked. No proxy tier needed for current access.

product_discovery.url_pattern_notes: Product URLs follow the pattern /{slug}/{code}.html where code is the product identifier (e.g., JI0079). The slug is the product name hyphenated. An optional query param forceSelSize can specify a size. Product code can be extracted from the URL to query the internal API at /api/products/{code}.

extraction_strategy.notes: The internal API at /api/products/{product_code} returns ~68KB of structured product data. This is the most reliable extraction method. The product code (e.g., JI0079) can be extracted from the URL path. Requires UC Chrome session to access the API endpoint.
extraction_strategy.jsonld_notes: The page contains 2 JSON-LD blocks: an OnlineStore type with merchant info and a ProductGroup type with product data (name, description, brand, aggregateRating, review, hasVariant). The ProductGroup schema is adidas's custom use - it groups product variants. Price data is not in the JSON-LD (offers=EMPTY), so CSS selectors or the internal API are needed for pricing.

css_selectors.notes: Common selectors (h1, price, availability, etc.) were not found in the probe's fast-scan. The site likely uses dynamic rendering with React/Vue components. A full browser DOM analysis via UC Chrome would be needed to identify specific selectors. The internal API is preferred over CSS scraping.

currency.source: adidas.ie is the Irish storefront, uses EUR

rate_limiting.justification: High anti-bot protection (Akamai). Conservative delay recommended to avoid triggering rate limits and session challenges.
